# Documentation

# Vercel Documentation

[Vercel Documentation](https://vercel.com/docs): Vercel is the AI Cloud - a unified platform for building, deploying, and scaling AI-powered applications and agentic workloads.

- [Getting Started](https://vercel.com/docs/getting-started-with-vercel): Install the Vercel CLI, add the Vercel Plugin or agent skills, and deploy your first project.
- [Fundamental Concepts](https://vercel.com/docs/fundamentals): Learn about the core concepts of Vercel
 - [Request Lifecycle](https://vercel.com/docs/fundamentals/infrastructure): Learn how Vercel routes, secures, and serves requests from your users to your application.
 - [Build System](https://vercel.com/docs/fundamentals/builds): Learn how Vercel transforms your source code into optimized assets ready to serve globally.
 - [What is Compute?](https://vercel.com/docs/fundamentals/what-is-compute): Learn about the different models for compute and how they can be used with Vercel.
- [Supported Frameworks](https://vercel.com/docs/frameworks): Vercel supports a wide range of the most popular frameworks, optimizing how your application builds and runs no matter what tool you use.
 - [Full-stack](https://vercel.com/docs/frameworks/full-stack): Vercel supports a wide range of the most popular backend frameworks, optimizing how your application builds and runs no matter what tooling you use.
 - [Next.js](https://vercel.com/docs/frameworks/full-stack/nextjs)
 - [SvelteKit](https://vercel.com/docs/frameworks/full-stack/sveltekit)

 - [Astro](https://vercel.com/docs/frameworks/frontend/astro)

- [Incremental Migration](https://vercel.com/docs/incremental-migration): Learn how to migrate your app or website to Vercel with minimal risk and high impact.
- [Production Checklist](https://vercel.com/docs/production-checklist): Ensure your application is ready for launch with this comprehensive production checklist by the Vercel engineering team. Covering operational excellence, security, reliability, performance efficiency, and cost optimization.

# Ship anything with Vercel

Deploy your app on Vercel in three steps: install the CLI, add agent support if you use an AI coding agent, and deploy.

[Get started guide](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/getting-started-with-vercel) [Get an API key](https://vercel.com/account/tokens) [API reference](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/rest-api)

TypeScriptPythoncURL

index.ts

```
import { generateText } from 'ai';
 
const { text } = await generateText({
 model: 'anthropic/claude-opus-4.7',
 prompt: 'What is the capital of France?',
});
```

index.py

```
import os
from openai import OpenAI
 
client = OpenAI(
 api_key=os.getenv('AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY'),
 base_url='https://ai-gateway.vercel.sh/v1'
)
 
response = client.chat.completions.create(
 model='openai/gpt-5.5',
 messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': 'Why is the sky blue?'}]
)
```

index.sh

```
curl -X POST "https://ai-gateway.vercel.sh/v1/chat/completions" \
-H "Authorization: Bearer $AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY" \
-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
-d '{
 "model": "openai/gpt-5.5",
 "messages": [
 { "role": "user", "content": "Why is the sky blue?" }
 ]
}'
```

Connect your [Git repository](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/git) to deploy on every push, with [automatic preview environments](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/deployments/environments#preview-environment-pre-production) for testing changes before production.

## [Build with AI](https://docs.vercel.com/#build-with-ai)

[**AI Gateway**\\ \\ Access hundreds of models through one endpoint with budgets, fallbacks, and monitoring.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/ai-gateway) [**eve**\\ \\ eve is a filesystem-first framework for building durable backend AI agents.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/eve) [**Agents**\\ \\ Build autonomous workflows and conversational interfaces with the AI SDK.](https://docs.vercel.com/kb/guide/how-to-build-ai-agents-with-vercel-and-the-ai-sdk) [**Sandbox**\\ \\ Run untrusted code in secure, ephemeral execution environments.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/sandbox)

## [Build your applications](https://docs.vercel.com/#build-your-applications)

Use one or more of the following tools to build your application depending on your needs:

[**Next.js**\\ \\ Build full-stack applications with Next.js or any of our supported frameworks.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/frameworks/nextjs) [**Functions**\\ \\ API routes with Fluid compute, active CPU, and provisioned memory — perfect for AI workloads.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/functions) [**Routing Middleware**\\ \\ Customize your application's behavior with code that runs before a request is processed.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/routing-middleware) [**Incremental Static Regeneration**\\ \\ Automatically regenerate your pages on a schedule or when a request is made.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/incremental-static-regeneration) [**Image Optimization**\\ \\ Optimize your images for the web.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/image-optimization) [**Manage Environments**\\ \\ Local, preview, production, and custom environments.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/deployments/environments)

## [Use Vercel's AI infrastructure](https://docs.vercel.com/#use-vercel's-ai-infrastructure)

Add intelligence to your applications with Vercel's AI-first infrastructure:

[**v0**\\ \\ Iterate on ideas with Vercel's AI-powered development assistant.](https://v0.app/docs/introduction) [**AI SDK**\\ \\ Integrate language models with streaming and tool calling.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/ai-sdk) [**AI Gateway**\\ \\ Route to any AI provider with automatic failover.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/ai-gateway) [**MCP Servers**\\ \\ Create tools for AI agents to interact with your systems.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/mcp) [**Agent Resources**\\ \\ Access documentation for AI tools, MCP servers, agent skills, and more.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/agent-resources) [**Claim Deployments**\\ \\ Allow AI agents to deploy a project and let a human take over.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/deployments/claim-deployments)

## [Secure your applications](https://docs.vercel.com/#secure-your-applications)

Secure your applications with the following tools:

[**Deployment Protection**\\ \\ Protect your applications from unauthorized access.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/deployment-protection) [**RBAC**\\ \\ Role-based access control for your applications.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/rbac) [**Configurable WAF**\\ \\ Customizable rules to protect against attacks, scrapers, and unwanted traffic.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/vercel-firewall/vercel-waf) [**Bot Management**\\ \\ Protect your applications from bots and automated traffic.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/bot-management) [**BotID**\\ \\ An invisible CAPTCHA that protects against sophisticated bots without challenges.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/botid) [**Platform DDoS Mitigation**\\ \\ Protect your applications from DDoS attacks.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/security/ddos-mitigation)

## [Collaborate with your team](https://docs.vercel.com/#collaborate-with-your-team)

Collaborate with your team using the following tools:

[**Toolbar**\\ \\ An in-browser toolbar to leave feedback, manage flags, preview drafts, and inspect performance.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/vercel-toolbar) [**Comments**\\ \\ Let teams and invited collaborators comment on your preview and production environments.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/comments) [**Draft Mode**\\ \\ View your unpublished headless CMS content on your site.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/draft-mode)

## [Deploy and scale](https://docs.vercel.com/#deploy-and-scale)

Vercel handles infrastructure automatically and provides these tools to help you deploy and scale:

[**Vercel Delivery Network**\\ \\ Fast, globally distributed execution.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/cdn) [**Rolling Releases**\\ \\ Roll out new deployments in increments.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/rolling-releases) [**Rollback Deployments**\\ \\ Roll back to a previous deployment for swift recovery from production incidents.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/instant-rollback) [**Observability Suite**\\ \\ Monitor performance and debug your AI workflows and apps.](https://docs.vercel.com/docs/observability)

## [Guides and tutorials](https://docs.vercel.com/#guides-and-tutorials)

Extend your knowledge with in-depth guides, videos, and tutorials on the [Vercel Knowledge Base](https://docs.vercel.com/kb):

[**Agent Stack**\\ \\ Build AI agents, integrate language models, and deploy AI-powered apps.](https://docs.vercel.com/kb/agent-stack) [**Backend**\\ \\ Server-side patterns, API routes, database connections, and compute.](https://docs.vercel.com/kb/backend) [**Services**\\ \\ Deploy multiple frontends and backends within a single Vercel project.](https://docs.vercel.com/kb/vercel-services) [**Domains**\\ \\ Manage domains and DNS records for your Vercel projects.](https://docs.vercel.com/kb/domains) [**Security**\\ \\ Protect your apps with authentication, firewall rules, and compliance guides.](https://docs.vercel.com/kb/security) [**CDN**\\ \\ Cache content globally, route requests, and run compute close to your data.](https://docs.vercel.com/kb/cdn) [**Integrations**\\ \\ Connect third-party tools, CMSs, and services to your Vercel project.](https://docs.vercel.com/kb/integrations) [**Migrations**\\ \\ Bring your existing apps, agents, and workflows to Vercel's agentic infrastructure.](https://docs.vercel.com/kb/migrations)

Vercel REST API

# [Using the REST API](https://api.vercel.com/#using-the-rest-api)

Interact programmatically with your Vercel account using the SDK or direct HTTP requests. You can deploy new versions of web applications, manage custom domains, retrieve information about deployments, and manage secrets and environment variables for projects.

The API supports any programming language or framework that can send HTTP requests.

## [API basics](https://api.vercel.com/#api-basics)

The API is exposed as an HTTP/1 and HTTP/2 service over SSL. All endpoints live under the URL `https://api.vercel.com` and follow the REST architecture.

## [Authentication](https://api.vercel.com/#authentication)

Vercel Access Tokens are required to authenticate and use the Vercel API. Include the token in the `Authorization` header:

`Authorization: Bearer `

Create and manage Access Tokens in your [account settings](https://vercel.com/account/tokens).

## [Accessing team resources](https://api.vercel.com/#accessing-team-resources)

By default, you can access resources in your personal account. To access resources owned by a team, append the Team ID as a query string:

`https://api.vercel.com/v6/deployments?teamId=[teamID]`

## [Rate limits](https://api.vercel.com/#rate-limits)

The API limits the number of calls you can make over a period of time. Rate limits are specified via response headers: `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, and `X-RateLimit-Reset`. See the [limits documentation](https://api.vercel.com/docs/limits#rate-limits) for details.

# Ship anything with Vercel

Deploy your app on Vercel in three steps: install the CLI, add agent support if you use an AI coding agent, and deploy.

[Get started guide](https://vercel.com/docs/getting-started-with-vercel) [Get an API key](https://vercel.com/account/tokens) [API reference](https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api)

TypeScriptPythoncURL

index.ts

```
import { generateText } from 'ai';
 
const { text } = await generateText({
 model: 'anthropic/claude-opus-4.7',
 prompt: 'What is the capital of France?',
});
```

index.py

```
import os
from openai import OpenAI
 
client = OpenAI(
 api_key=os.getenv('AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY'),
 base_url='https://ai-gateway.vercel.sh/v1'
)
 
response = client.chat.completions.create(
 model='openai/gpt-5.5',
 messages=[{'role': 'user', 'content': 'Why is the sky blue?'}]
)
```

index.sh

```
curl -X POST "https://ai-gateway.vercel.sh/v1/chat/completions" \
-H "Authorization: Bearer $AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY" \
-H "Content-Type: application/json" \
-d '{
 "model": "openai/gpt-5.5",
 "messages": [
 { "role": "user", "content": "Why is the sky blue?" }
 ]
}'
```

Connect your [Git repository](https://vercel.com/docs/git) to deploy on every push, with [automatic preview environments](https://vercel.com/docs/deployments/environments#preview-environment-pre-production) for testing changes before production.

## [Build with AI](https://vercel.com/#build-with-ai)

[**AI Gateway**\\ \\ Access hundreds of models through one endpoint with budgets, fallbacks, and monitoring.](https://vercel.com/docs/ai-gateway) [**eve**\\ \\ eve is a filesystem-first framework for building durable backend AI agents.](https://vercel.com/docs/eve) [**Agents**\\ \\ Build autonomous workflows and conversational interfaces with the AI SDK.](https://vercel.com/kb/guide/how-to-build-ai-agents-with-vercel-and-the-ai-sdk) [**Sandbox**\\ \\ Run untrusted code in secure, ephemeral execution environments.](https://vercel.com/docs/sandbox)

## [Build your applications](https://vercel.com/#build-your-applications)

Use one or more of the following tools to build your application depending on your needs:

[**Next.js**\\ \\ Build full-stack applications with Next.js or any of our supported frameworks.](https://vercel.com/docs/frameworks/nextjs) [**Functions**\\ \\ API routes with Fluid compute, active CPU, and provisioned memory — perfect for AI workloads.](https://vercel.com/docs/functions) [**Routing Middleware**\\ \\ Customize your application's behavior with code that runs before a request is processed.](https://vercel.com/docs/routing-middleware) [**Incremental Static Regeneration**\\ \\ Automatically regenerate your pages on a schedule or when a request is made.](https://vercel.com/docs/incremental-static-regeneration) [**Image Optimization**\\ \\ Optimize your images for the web.](https://vercel.com/docs/image-optimization) [**Manage Environments**\\ \\ Local, preview, production, and custom environments.](https://vercel.com/docs/deployments/environments)

## [Use Vercel's AI infrastructure](https://vercel.com/#use-vercel's-ai-infrastructure)

Add intelligence to your applications with Vercel's AI-first infrastructure:

[**v0**\\ \\ Iterate on ideas with Vercel's AI-powered development assistant.](https://v0.app/docs/introduction) [**AI SDK**\\ \\ Integrate language models with streaming and tool calling.](https://vercel.com/docs/ai-sdk) [**AI Gateway**\\ \\ Route to any AI provider with automatic failover.](https://vercel.com/docs/ai-gateway) [**MCP Servers**\\ \\ Create tools for AI agents to interact with your systems.](https://vercel.com/docs/mcp) [**Agent Resources**\\ \\ Access documentation for AI tools, MCP servers, agent skills, and more.](https://vercel.com/docs/agent-resources) [**Claim Deployments**\\ \\ Allow AI agents to deploy a project and let a human take over.](https://vercel.com/docs/deployments/claim-deployments)

## [Secure your applications](https://vercel.com/#secure-your-applications)

Secure your applications with the following tools:

[**Deployment Protection**\\ \\ Protect your applications from unauthorized access.](https://vercel.com/docs/deployment-protection) [**RBAC**\\ \\ Role-based access control for your applications.](https://vercel.com/docs/rbac) [**Configurable WAF**\\ \\ Customizable rules to protect against attacks, scrapers, and unwanted traffic.](https://vercel.com/docs/vercel-firewall/vercel-waf) [**Bot Management**\\ \\ Protect your applications from bots and automated traffic.](https://vercel.com/docs/bot-management) [**BotID**\\ \\ An invisible CAPTCHA that protects against sophisticated bots without challenges.](https://vercel.com/docs/botid) [**Platform DDoS Mitigation**\\ \\ Protect your applications from DDoS attacks.](https://vercel.com/docs/security/ddos-mitigation)

## [Collaborate with your team](https://vercel.com/#collaborate-with-your-team)

Collaborate with your team using the following tools:

[**Toolbar**\\ \\ An in-browser toolbar to leave feedback, manage flags, preview drafts, and inspect performance.](https://vercel.com/docs/vercel-toolbar) [**Comments**\\ \\ Let teams and invited collaborators comment on your preview and production environments.](https://vercel.com/docs/comments) [**Draft Mode**\\ \\ View your unpublished headless CMS content on your site.](https://vercel.com/docs/draft-mode)

## [Deploy and scale](https://vercel.com/#deploy-and-scale)

Vercel handles infrastructure automatically and provides these tools to help you deploy and scale:

[**Vercel Delivery Network**\\ \\ Fast, globally distributed execution.](https://vercel.com/docs/cdn) [**Rolling Releases**\\ \\ Roll out new deployments in increments.](https://vercel.com/docs/rolling-releases) [**Rollback Deployments**\\ \\ Roll back to a previous deployment for swift recovery from production incidents.](https://vercel.com/docs/instant-rollback) [**Observability Suite**\\ \\ Monitor performance and debug your AI workflows and apps.](https://vercel.com/docs/observability)

## [Guides and tutorials](https://vercel.com/#guides-and-tutorials)

Extend your knowledge with in-depth guides, videos, and tutorials on the [Vercel Knowledge Base](https://vercel.com/kb):

[**Agent Stack**\\ \\ Build AI agents, integrate language models, and deploy AI-powered apps.](https://vercel.com/kb/agent-stack) [**Backend**\\ \\ Server-side patterns, API routes, database connections, and compute.](https://vercel.com/kb/backend) [**Services**\\ \\ Deploy multiple frontends and backends within a single Vercel project.](https://vercel.com/kb/vercel-services) [**Domains**\\ \\ Manage domains and DNS records for your Vercel projects.](https://vercel.com/kb/domains) [**Security**\\ \\ Protect your apps with authentication, firewall rules, and compliance guides.](https://vercel.com/kb/security) [**CDN**\\ \\ Cache content globally, route requests, and run compute close to your data.](https://vercel.com/kb/cdn) [**Integrations**\\ \\ Connect third-party tools, CMSs, and services to your Vercel project.](https://vercel.com/kb/integrations) [**Migrations**\\ \\ Bring your existing apps, agents, and workflows to Vercel's agentic infrastructure.](https://vercel.com/kb/migrations)

Vercel REST API

# [Using the REST API](https://vercel.com/#using-the-rest-api)

Interact programmatically with your Vercel account using the SDK or direct HTTP requests. You can deploy new versions of web applications, manage custom domains, retrieve information about deployments, and manage secrets and environment variables for projects.

The API supports any programming language or framework that can send HTTP requests.

## [API basics](https://vercel.com/#api-basics)

The API is exposed as an HTTP/1 and HTTP/2 service over SSL. All endpoints live under the URL `https://api.vercel.com` and follow the REST architecture.

## [Authentication](https://vercel.com/#authentication)

Vercel Access Tokens are required to authenticate and use the Vercel API. Include the token in the `Authorization` header:

`Authorization: Bearer `

Create and manage Access Tokens in your [account settings](https://vercel.com/account/tokens).

## [Accessing team resources](https://vercel.com/#accessing-team-resources)

By default, you can access resources in your personal account. To access resources owned by a team, append the Team ID as a query string:

`https://api.vercel.com/v6/deployments?teamId=[teamID]`

## [Rate limits](https://vercel.com/#rate-limits)

The API limits the number of calls you can make over a period of time. Rate limits are specified via response headers: `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, and `X-RateLimit-Reset`. See the [limits documentation](https://vercel.com/docs/limits#rate-limits) for details.

## [Endpoints](https://vercel.com/#endpoints)

Browse all available REST API endpoints grouped by category.

[Access-groups11 endpoints](https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/access-groups/reads-an-access-group) [Aliases6 endpoints](https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/aliases/list-deployment-aliases) [Api-observability2 endpoints](https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/api-observability/lists-disabled-observability-plus-projects) [Artifacts6 endpoints](https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/artifacts/record-an-artifacts-cache-usage-event) [Authentication5 endpoints](https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/authentication/sso-token-exchange) [Billing3 endpoints](https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/billing/list-focus-billing-charges) [Bulk-redirects7 endpoints](https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/bulk-redirects/gets-project-level-redirects) [Certs4 endpoints](https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/certs/get-cert-by-id) [Checks-v210 endpoints](https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/checks-v2/list-all-checks-for-a-project) [Connect4 endpoints](https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/connect/create-a-connector) [Deployments10 endpoints](https://vercel.com/docs/rest-api/deployments/get-deployment-events) [DNS4 Vercel REST API

# [Using the REST API](https://vercel.com/#using-the-rest-api)

Interact programmatically with your Vercel account using the SDK or direct HTTP requests. You can deploy new versions of web applications, manage custom domains, retrieve information about deployments, and manage secrets and environment variables for projects.

The API supports any programming language or framework that can send HTTP requests.

## [API basics](https://vercel.com/#api-basics)

The API is exposed as an HTTP/1 and HTTP/2 service over SSL. All endpoints live under the URL `https://api.vercel.com` and follow the REST architecture.

## [Authentication](https://vercel.com/#authentication)

Vercel Access Tokens are required to authenticate and use the Vercel API. Include the token in the `Authorization` header:

`Authorization: Bearer `

Create and manage Access Tokens in your [account settings](https://vercel.com/account/tokens).

## [Accessing team resources](https://vercel.com/#accessing-team-resources)

By default, you can access resources in your personal account. To access resources owned by a team, append the Team ID as a query string:

`https://api.vercel.com/v6/deployments?teamId=[teamID]`

## [Rate limits](https://vercel.com/#rate-limits)

The API limits the number of calls you can make over a period of time. Rate limits are specified via response headers: `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, and `X-RateLimit-Reset`. See the [limits documentation](https://vercel.com/docs/limits#rate-limits) for details.

## [Endpoints](https://vercel.com/#endpoints)
